_meta.dna: oauth3(governance) = scope(fine) x ttl(expire) x revoke(instant) x budget(enforce) x stepup(approve) x audit(chain) x consent(explicit)
_meta.question_expansion_instruction: Each floor has 7 seed questions. Questions must be EXECUTABLE — answerable by checking if something actually works, not theoretical capability.

name: Tower of OAuth3 Governance
protocol: FORECAST -> CROSS-REF -> AUDIT(7q) -> FIX -> VERIFY -> SEAL -> POSTMORTEM

floors.persona_context: 44, Tokyo, CISO at a financial services firm. Expert tech level. Needs to define fine-grained OAuth3 scopes so each AI agent can only access the exact resources it needs — no more, no less. Zero tolerance for over-permissioned tokens.
questions: Can I define a scope that limits an agent to reading Gmail but not sending emails, with that distinction enforced at the token level?
Does the scope definition UI show me a tree of available permissions so I can select exactly which actions to allow?
Can I create a compound scope like 'read:gmail + write:google-drive/reports-folder' that spans multiple services with path-level granularity?
Does the system reject an agent's attempt to perform an action outside its granted scope with a clear error message showing which scope is missing?
Can I view all active scopes granted to all agents in a single dashboard sorted by permission breadth?
Does the scope format follow a documented schema that I can version-control and review in a pull request?
Can I create a scope template (like 'read-only-researcher') that I can assign to multiple agents without redefining permissions each time?
floors.question_expansion_instruction: Expand from seed questions to cover all scope definition edge cases. Kenji is CISO — he needs granular, auditable, reviewable scopes. Over-permissioning is a fireable offense in his role. Every scope must be provably minimal.

floors.persona_context: 37, Riyadh, Security architect at a government contractor. Expert tech level. Requires that all agent tokens expire automatically — no permanent tokens, no forgotten sessions, mandatory re-authentication after TTL expires.
questions: Can I set a TTL of 4 hours on an agent's Gmail access token so it expires automatically even if the agent is still running?
Does the system show me a countdown of remaining TTL for each active token in the session dashboard?
When a token expires mid-task, does the agent pause and request re-authorization instead of failing silently?
Can I set different TTLs for different scopes — for example, 1 hour for email send but 8 hours for email read?
Does the system prevent me from setting a TTL longer than 24 hours for any scope (hard maximum)?
Can I see an audit log of all token expirations showing when each token was created, its TTL, and when it actually expired?
Does the system alert me if an agent is approaching token expiry during a long-running task so I can re-authorize proactively?
floors.question_expansion_instruction: Expand from seed questions to cover all TTL enforcement scenarios. Fatima works for a government contractor — perpetual tokens are a security violation. Every token must have a bounded lifetime, and expiry must be enforced server-side, not client-side.

floors.persona_context: 48, Stockholm, IT security manager at a multinational corporation. Expert tech level. An employee just left the company — needs to revoke all their agent tokens instantly across all services, all sessions, all devices.
questions: Can I revoke all tokens for a specific user account with a single action and have it take effect within 5 seconds?
Does the revocation dashboard show me confirmation that each token was actually invalidated, not just marked for deletion?
If an agent is mid-execution when I revoke its token, does the agent stop immediately rather than completing the current step?
Can I revoke a specific scope without revoking the entire token — for example, removing Gmail access while keeping Drive access?
Does the system maintain a revocation log showing who revoked which token, when, and the reason they entered?
Can I set up automatic revocation rules — for example, revoke all tokens if a user fails 3 consecutive login attempts?
After revocation, if the agent tries to use the revoked token, does the error response reveal nothing about why it was revoked (preventing information leakage)?
floors.question_expansion_instruction: Expand from seed questions to cover all instant revocation scenarios. Henrik is handling an employee departure — revocation must be instant, complete, auditable, and irreversible. Any delay between revocation and enforcement is a security breach.

floors.persona_context: 32, Nairobi, Finance director at an NGO. Intermediate tech level. Manages a strict $500/month AI tool budget across 12 field workers. Needs hard spending limits that cannot be bypassed, with alerts before limits are reached.
questions: Can I set a hard budget of $40/month per field worker that physically blocks recipe execution when exceeded?
Does the budget dashboard show real-time spend per user with a visual gauge showing percentage consumed?
When a user hits 80% of their budget, does the system send an email alert to both the user and me as the admin?
Can I set a per-recipe cost ceiling — for example, block any single recipe execution that would cost more than $5?
Does the system show me a projected month-end spend based on current run rate so I can intervene early?
Can I transfer unused budget from one user to another mid-month without resetting their usage history?
Does the budget enforcement work at the token level (pre-execution check) rather than post-execution billing reconciliation?
floors.question_expansion_instruction: Expand from seed questions to cover all budget gate scenarios. Grace manages an NGO — every dollar is accountable to donors. Budget enforcement must be pre-execution (fail-closed), not post-execution (surprise bills). Alert thresholds must be configurable.

floors.persona_context: 51, Toronto, Chief Medical Officer at a hospital network. Expert tech level. AI agents assist with patient scheduling and report generation. Any action that touches patient data must trigger step-up approval — the agent pauses and a human doctor approves.
questions: When an agent attempts to access a patient record, does the system pause execution and send me a step-up approval request showing exactly what data will be accessed?
Does the approval dialog show the patient name, record type, and specific fields the agent wants to read — not just 'access patient data'?
Can I configure step-up rules by data sensitivity — for example, demographics auto-approve but diagnosis requires manual approval?
Does the system enforce a timeout on step-up requests — if I don't approve within 15 minutes, the request is automatically denied?
Can I delegate step-up approval to specific colleagues during my off-hours without giving them full admin access?
Does the audit trail record who approved each step-up request, when, from which device, and the IP address?
If I deny a step-up request, does the agent receive a clear rejection with the option to proceed with a modified scope that excludes the sensitive data?
floors.question_expansion_instruction: Expand from seed questions to cover all step-up approval scenarios. Dr. Singh handles HIPAA-protected data — step-up must be mandatory, auditable, time-bounded, and show exactly what the agent wants to do. Auto-approve on sensitive data is a compliance violation.

floors.persona_context: 43, Madrid, Head of Internal Audit at a European bank. Expert tech level. Needs a complete, immutable audit chain of every permission granted, every action taken, every token issued and revoked — for ECB regulatory reporting.
questions: Can I query the audit chain for all actions taken by a specific agent in a specific date range and export the results?
Does every entry in the audit chain include a SHA-256 hash linking it to the previous entry, forming a tamper-evident chain?
Can I verify the integrity of the entire audit chain with a single command that checks every hash link?
Does the audit chain capture permission changes — when a scope was added, modified, or revoked, and by whom?
Can I generate an ECB-compliant audit report directly from the chain without manual data transformation?
Does the audit chain survive a server restart — is it persisted to durable storage, not held in memory?
If an auditor finds a gap in the chain (missing entries), does the system flag it as a potential tampering event?
floors.question_expansion_instruction: Expand from seed questions to cover all audit chain scenarios. Lucia reports to the ECB — the chain must be immutable, verifiable, queryable, and exportable. Any gap or modification in the chain is a regulatory incident.

floors.persona_context: 62, Copenhagen, Data protection officer at a logistics company. Intermediate tech level. GDPR requires explicit, informed consent before any AI agent processes personal data. Needs a consent UI that non-technical warehouse staff can understand and use.
questions: Does the consent dialog explain in plain language what the agent will do, what data it will access, and for how long — without technical jargon?
Can a warehouse worker who has never used a terminal understand the consent dialog and make an informed yes/no decision?
Does the consent record capture the exact text shown to the user, their response, the timestamp, and their user ID for GDPR accountability?
Can a user withdraw consent after granting it, and does withdrawal immediately stop all processing of their data?
Does the consent UI support multiple languages so my warehouse staff in Poland, Spain, and Germany each see consent in their native language?
Can I view a dashboard showing all active consents, pending consents, and withdrawn consents across my organization?
Does the system refuse to proceed if consent was not explicitly granted — no pre-checked boxes, no implied consent from inaction?
floors.question_expansion_instruction: Expand from seed questions to cover all consent UI scenarios. Peter is a DPO — GDPR requires informed, explicit, withdrawable consent. The UI must work for non-technical staff. Pre-checked boxes or implied consent are legal violations.
